source_alignment.subject: English
source_alignment.source_statement: Use devices to build cohesion within a paragraph; link ideas across paragraphs using adverbials of time, place and number or tense choices.
source_alignment.derived_objective_note: This pack treats cohesion as reader guidance, developing clear reference chains, purposeful repetition, meaning-matched adverbials and consistent tense links without encouraging formulaic overlinking.

objective.child_goal: I can connect sentences and paragraphs so the reader can follow each idea.
objective.statement: Select and edit reference, repetition, adverbial and tense links to create clear cohesion within and across paragraphs.
misconceptions: Adds a conjunction or adverbial to every sentence even when the meaning relationship is unclear.
Uses pronouns or determiners whose referent could be more than one noun or idea.
Avoids all repetition by replacing a precise key term with confusing synonyms.
Changes tense between paragraphs without a deliberate time shift.

vocabulary.child_explanation: The links that help writing hold together and make sense.
vocabulary.audio_script: Cohesion guides the reader from one idea to the next.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: Sentence and paragraph cards joined by labelled threads.

vocabulary.child_explanation: A word or phrase that points to something already named or about to be named.
vocabulary.audio_script: A reference word must point clearly to the right person, object or idea.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: An arrow from this, they or it to its matching noun phrase.

vocabulary.child_explanation: A word or phrase that shows how an idea connects in time, place or order.
vocabulary.audio_script: A cohesive adverbial is a signpost chosen for the relationship between ideas.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: Signposts labelled Later, Nearby and Secondly between paragraph cards.

vocabulary.child_explanation: The verb form that locates an action in time.
vocabulary.audio_script: Tense choices can keep a time sequence clear or show a deliberate shift.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: Past and present timeline tracks beneath verb phrases.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Follow each reference arrow. What does this, it or they point to?
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Activate antecedent tracking before learners edit longer passages.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: A clear reference has one sensible destination.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Reference words and noun phrases appear on cards connected by selectable arrows.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Build a route the reader can follow through repeated ideas, clear references and useful signposts.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Frame cohesion as meaning and reader orientation rather than adding connectives mechanically.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Good links show what continues, changes, happens next or moves elsewhere.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: A paragraph route uses threads labelled topic, reference, time, place and order.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: The roof garden collected rainwater. This supply fed the planters. Later that week, the seedlings opened.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Model a precise noun-to-determiner reference and a time adverbial chosen for a genuine shift.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: This supply points clearly to rainwater. Later that week moves the reader forward in time.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: One reference thread and one timeline signpost are added while unnecessary link cards stay unused.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Order the paragraph cards, connect each reference and edit only the links that help the reader.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Coordinate sequencing, reference clarity, lexical cohesion and adverbial choice.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Reread after every edit. A link should make the relationship clearer, not merely make the sentence longer.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: A split editor shows paragraph cards, reference arrows and a read-aloud flow check.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Mara placed the model beside the lamp before moving it. What could it mean, and how can the sentence be repaired?
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Detect ambiguous pronoun reference and select a proportionate repair.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: If a reference arrow can point to two nouns, repeat the important noun or rewrite the sentence.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: The pronoun arrow splits toward model and lamp until the learner edits the noun phrase.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Teach your companion why firstly, meanwhile and nearby cannot simply swap places.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Gather metalinguistic evidence that cohesive devices encode different relationships.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Explain whether each signpost shows order, simultaneous time or place.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Three signposts are tested on the same paragraph gap and mapped to different relationship icons.

manipulatives.purpose: Make noun, pronoun, determiner and lexical repetition chains visible within a paragraph.
manipulatives.interaction_notes: Learners select a reference expression, connect it to a referent, compare precise repetition with synonym choices and edit ambiguous chains.
manipulatives.accessibility_notes: Connections are represented in both a diagram and a text table; keyboard commands cycle possible referents; no colour-only coding is used; line focus and read-aloud are available; and reference density can be reduced to one chain at a time.

manipulatives.purpose: Sequence paragraphs and choose time, place, number or tense links that match the intended relationship.
manipulatives.interaction_notes: Learners order paragraph summaries, insert optional signpost cards, inspect verb tense tracks and compare the passage before and after editing.
manipulatives.accessibility_notes: Cards have move-up/down controls, headings remain visible, each signpost includes a relationship label, tense changes are announced, and a plain numbered-list mode provides the complete task without dragging.

practice.interleaving_notes: Secure clear sentence-to-sentence reference first, then mix lexical chains and meaning-matched adverbials; introduce cross-paragraph tense links and deliberately sparse cohesion only after learners can explain each link's purpose.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Track and repair pronouns and determiners so every reference has a clear referent.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: 3-5 sentence paragraph + noun phrases + pronoun/determiner references + link or repair task
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Include singular/plural and human/non-human references without relying on gender stereotypes or obscure names.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Balance necessary key-term repetition with clear paraphrase and reference.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: technical or narrative paragraph + repeated key noun + precise/ambiguous replacement choices + reread check
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Do not imply repetition is always weak; retain exact terminology where synonym substitution would alter meaning.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Choose time, place and number adverbials that accurately signal the relationship between paragraphs.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: ordered paragraph summaries + gap + relationship goal + signpost choices + optional no-link choice
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Include cases where no explicit adverbial is the clearest option; punctuation and register must fit the passage.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Maintain tense across paragraphs or mark a deliberate earlier/later time relationship.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: two-paragraph passage + verb highlights + timeline + choose or edit tense sequence + explain time effect
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Distinguish cohesion-focused tense choices from broad grammar correction; accept deliberate shifts when clearly cued.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Improve writing that overuses repetitive or mismatched cohesive devices.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: overlinked passage + remove/replace/retain decisions + compare read-aloud flow + explain reader effect
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Teacher review should preserve author voice and allow more than one effective edit where justified.

body.prompt: In the paragraph, what does 'This supply' refer to?
body.text: The roof garden collected rainwater in a large tank. This supply fed the planters during dry weather.

expected_answer.value: rainwater in the tank

hints: Ask what could be used to feed the planters.
Follow the reference to the nearest sensible idea, not simply the nearest noun.
question_variants.explanation: 'This supply' refers to the collected rainwater stored in the tank.

body.prompt: Repair the unclear reference: 'Mara placed the model beside the lamp before moving it closer to the wall.' Mara moved the model.
body.text: Mara placed the model beside the lamp before moving it closer to the wall.
choices: Mara placed the model beside the lamp before moving the model closer to the wall.
Mara placed the model beside the lamp before moving it closer.
Mara placed it beside it before moving it.
Mara placed the model, and it was beside the lamp and it moved.

expected_answer.value: Mara placed the model beside the lamp before moving the model closer to the wall.

hints: The original 'it' could point to the model or the lamp.
Repeat the intended noun once to make the meaning clear.
question_variants.explanation: Repeating 'the model' removes the ambiguity and tells the reader exactly what Mara moved.

body.prompt: Choose the best opening for the second paragraph. Paragraph 1 explains the first safety check. Paragraph 2 explains the next check.

expected_answer.value: Secondly,

hints: The paragraphs are arranged as steps in order.
Choose a signpost that shows number or sequence.
question_variants.explanation: 'Secondly' tells the reader that the next paragraph gives the second step in an ordered explanation.

body.prompt: Choose the sentence that clearly shows the discovery happened before the team returned.
choices: The team returned to base. Earlier, they had discovered a narrow tunnel.
The team returned to base. Earlier, they discover a narrow tunnel.
The team returns to base. Earlier, they will discover a narrow tunnel.
The team returned to base. Nearby, they had discovered tomorrow.

expected_answer.value: The team returned to base. Earlier, they had discovered a narrow tunnel.

hints: The discovery happened first.
Use 'Earlier' and 'had discovered' to look back from a past event.
question_variants.explanation: 'Earlier' and the past perfect 'had discovered' make the earlier event clear while the main narrative remains in the past.

body.paragraph_two: They changed the plan and inspected the remaining equipment.

gamification.purpose: restore one reader-route connection after explaining a real cohesion link
gamification.reward: one calm route thread or editor-journal stamp
gamification.retry_message: That edit gives us useful reader evidence. Restore the original, test one link, and try again.

expected_answer.value: For this reason,

hints: Name the relationship in plain words before choosing a link.
Reread both paragraphs with the choice; reject a link that changes their logic.
question_variants.explanation: 'For this reason,' accurately signals cause and result. It helps the reader connect the paragraph ideas without adding a relationship the content does not support.

feedback.correct: The reader route is clear. 'For this reason,' accurately signals cause and result. It helps the reader connect the paragraph ideas without adding a relationship the content does not support.
feedback.repair: Label the relationship as cause and result, then compare the passage with and without an explicit signpost.
feedback.meaning_check: Compare the original and chosen version: the people, actions, sequence and relationship should remain the same unless the task explicitly repairs one of them.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Adds a conjunction or adverbial to every sentence even when the meaning relationship is unclear.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: The learner inserts repeated or mismatched links and cannot state whether they show time, place, order, cause, contrast or continuation.
repair_sequence: Remove all optional link words and reread the passage.
Name the relationship at each point where the reader genuinely needs guidance.
Add one matching device or choose no explicit link, then compare the flow.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: The learner retains, removes and replaces links selectively in a fresh paragraph and explains each decision.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Uses pronouns or determiners whose referent could be more than one noun or idea.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: A reference map has two plausible destinations or the learner identifies a different referent from the intended meaning.
repair_sequence: Repeat the important noun or restructure the sentence.
Reread from the previous sentence and confirm only one arrow remains.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: The learner independently repairs an ambiguous reference without replacing every pronoun.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Avoids all repetition by replacing a precise key term with confusing synonyms.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: A technical or central noun is replaced by a vague or inaccurate alternative that weakens the topic chain.
repair_sequence: Underline the key term whose exact meaning must stay stable.
Compare precise repetition, pronoun reference and a proposed synonym.
Choose the option that preserves meaning and keeps the referent clear.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: The learner keeps necessary repetition in a new explanation and varies only language that remains precise.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Changes tense between paragraphs without a deliberate time shift.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: Verb forms move between past and present while the timeline and viewpoint remain unchanged.
repair_sequence: Place the events on a simple timeline.
Choose the main narrative or explanation tense.
Edit verbs to match, retaining a different tense only where a signalled time relationship requires it.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: The learner maintains tense across a fresh passage or explains a purposeful earlier-time shift.

adaptive_support.low_sensory: Use a calm editing desk, static paragraph cards, one visible thread family at a time and no decorative page movement.
adaptive_support.reduced_motion: Replace drawing and snapping threads with persistent numbered reference labels, before/after passage panels and user-controlled focus changes.
adaptive_support.audio_first: Optional whole-passage and sentence playback uses only ElevenLabs assets after human listening approval. Browser TTS is prohibited; if audio is unavailable, visible text, line focus and adult/partner reading routes remain complete.
adaptive_support.reading_support: Provide line focus, adjustable spacing, paragraph summaries, optional vocabulary glosses and a text-list alternative to visual thread maps.
adaptive_support.attention_support: Use a fixed ORDER-REFERENCE-SIGNPOST-REREAD sequence and reveal only one editing decision while keeping the source passage available.
adaptive_support.processing_support: Remove timers, preserve edits during rereading, allow undo and offer oral, typed or structured-choice explanations.
adaptive_support.confidence_support: Frame every task as reader testing, preserve effective original wording and show that removing a link can be a successful edit.
adaptive_support.eal_support: Preteach reference words and time, place and order adverbials in short paired examples; allow oral rehearsal and discuss when exact noun repetition supports clarity.

evidence.mastery_rule: Expected requires clear reference and a meaning-matched link within and across paragraphs in at least two formats. Secure requires selective editing, deliberate tense control and explanation of reader effect on two retained sessions.
evidence.parent_summary: Can link sentences and paragraphs clearly using references, repeated ideas, signposts and consistent tense.
evidence.teacher_evidence: Stores paragraph order, reference links, device choices, tense edits and explanations separately so cohesion knowledge is not hidden by handwriting or transcription demand.
next_actions: Route ambiguous reference to one-chain-at-a-time mapping.
Apply secure cohesion choices to the learner's own explanation and narrative drafts.
Schedule mixed reference, adverbial and tense review at 1, 3, 7, 14 and 30 days.

qa.notes: Wave-six review bank reaches the 240-item pilot target with four preserved curated questions and 236 deterministic candidates covering paragraph links, reference chains, pronouns and determiners, adverbials, repetition and substitution, sequencing, and meaning-preserving cohesion editing. SEND/dyslexia scaffolds, supported non-drag interactions, rich repair feedback, low-pressure reader-route progress and optional ElevenLabs references are included. Audio requires human listening approval and browser TTS is prohibited; independent English, teacher, SEND, accessibility, safeguarding, audio and renderer review remains required before promotion.
